snippets.title: Gradient descent with different fixed learning rates
snippets.explanation: For L(w)=(w−3)², η=0.05 converges smoothly, η=0.5 converges faster, η=1.1 oscillates/diverges because it exceeds the stability bound 2/L=1 for this quadratic.

snippets.title: Step learning rate schedule in PyTorch
snippets.code:
import torch
from torch.optim import SGD
from torch.optim.lr_scheduler import StepLR

model = torch.nn.Linear(10, 1)
optimizer = SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1)
scheduler = StepLR(optimizer, step_size=30, gamma=0.1)

for epoch in range(100):
    train_one_epoch(model, optimizer)  # uses optimizer.param_groups[0]['lr']
    scheduler.step()
    print(epoch, optimizer.param_groups[0]['lr'])
snippets.explanation: η starts at 0.1; every 30 epochs it is multiplied by 0.1. The training loop reads the current lr from the optimizer after each scheduler step.

snippets.title: Log-scale learning rate sweep
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

learning_rates = np.logspace(-4, 0, 5)  # 1e-4 ... 1.0
results = {}
for eta in learning_rates:
    loss_final = train_and_return_loss(lr=eta, epochs=5)
    results[float(eta)] = loss_final

best_eta = min(results, key=results.get)
print("best lr:", best_eta, "loss:", results[best_eta])
snippets.explanation: Sweeping η on a log grid is standard practice: compare final validation loss across orders of magnitude before fine-tuning.
